name: The White House
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2725233c0150fdd009b41132514ba8b0be49d1ce6b651fca3a769eeae2570e27a90200633ca5e7451973a28b93816c481c6e5a1705bdfd77
President Trump: America's Economy Is Back and Roaring and Its People Are Winning

- USA Today

“We are changing Tax Day for Americans across the country,” President Donald J. Trump wrote in an op-ed this week. “This is the last year Americans will fill out outdated, complicated tax forms. In the years ahead, because I signed one of the largest tax cuts in history and the most sweeping tax reform in a generation, many Americans will complete their taxes on a simple, single sheet of paper.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2725233c0150fdd08974dd8eab0870ac3a3822bbf15d33285fa6e8958f3feddd6e33bc945bcc49ac6205ee8493dd7893622b239fa4768d51

Donald Trump Vows to Stay Out of TPP Unless ‘They Offer Us a Deal That We Cannot Refuse’

- Breitbart

President Trump assured Americans on Wednesday that he would not reconsider joining the Trans-Pacific Partnership unless other countries offered America irresistible terms. “They’re all calling, wanting to make a deal, but we think that’s much better for us,” the President said.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2725233c0150fdd00245ae3b22e12a8eb127cc81df130b34b29adb593c0078b1e480d52e421485e1b1c81dc5ba9ab47fe177e24ede31c8f9

'Thank You For Your Service:' Surgeon General's Uniformed Corps Battles Opioid Epidemic

- USA Today

This week, Surgeon General Jerome Adams visited the “Prescribed to Death” opioid memorial near the White House with several members of the U.S. Public Health Service’s Commissioned Corps. The Corps often “get mistaken for military officers. But they can rightly accept the praise when strangers say, ‘Thank you for your service’” for their work serving America’s most vulnerable, Jayne O’Donnell writes.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2725233c0150fdd09a7dab1fd11558b6b505cbf2c2bec7085f27adab11f67667eea2be9108025fd31229f4f407199f276ae4b1efb79812ae

DHS Nets 225 Deportation Targets in New York Sweep

- The Washington Times

U.S. Department of Homeland Security officials announced Tuesday that they had arrested 225 immigrants in a “targeted sweep in New York City and the surrounding area,” Stephen Dinan reports. Among those taken into custody were criminals with rape, manslaughter, and firearms-related convictions.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2725233c0150fdd039246d26e755e2f56eb481534533b51b3e4ded76b68f5333a992e1d321cb6269ddef71ee4b5a3e1d56581f6373c5ee17

California Reaches Deal with Trump Administration on National Guard Role

- The Washington Post

Gov. Jerry Brown (D-CA) “will deploy 400 soldiers who will work against drug trafficking and cross-border criminal groups” following negotiations with the Trump Administration, The Washington Post writes. This deal means the President has “the full participation of every border state in his plan to reinforce federal agents with Guard troops” to protect the southern U.S. border.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2725233c0150fdd0b737bc8f5b5bc106e01e5e5221b04d914aab30238526506e91e05b3caad461fe2c3bcb82c1a325d253a29dcb32637bc8

Feds Expose Network That Smuggled Illegal Immigrants from Syria to U.S.

- The Washington Times

“The Treasury Department slapped sanctions Wednesday on a Syrian man and his criminal syndicate, blaming them for smuggling ‘hundreds’ of illegal immigrants from Syria and Lebanon into Mexico and then helping them to jump the border into the U.S.,” The Washington Times reports.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2725233c0150fdd068ef6e51a905d4ae254c7906ac24d32522aa375cce26a668f845b042a332786fb0d3f2172a2eecfbf3efe5617cb59187

name: Congressman Tim Ryan
subject: Why I'm endorsing J.D. Scholten
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
Friends,
Immediately after the inspiring March for Our Lives, right-wing Republican Congressman Steve King of Iowa decided to go online and mock Parkland shooting survivor and gun violence prevention leader Emma Gonzalez.

It was the latest in Steve King's career-long effort to offend or disrespect as many Americans as humanly possible -- all while voting in lockstep with the Republican Party's "billionaires-first" agenda.

But in 2018, karma is catching up to Steve King. His opponent is fifth-generation Iowan and working-class champion J.D. Scholten -- who is running a bold, grassroots campaign with the slogan "Standing Tall for All."

I'm proud to endorse J.D. Scholten for Congress in Iowa's 4th District -- [**_can you join me by chipping to elect J.D. and take back the House?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1082410/6060207/1808354944?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4M2EwNTkyNS04MTQ1LWU4MTEtODBjMy0wMDE1NWRhNzg1ZTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HHGURJ3gZUyjIgY34E2MCPHZHkX3aIqKXT3s0P4cV9k=)
An accomplished athlete who has played baseball in seven countries, J.D. is indeed "standing tall" -- at 6 feet, 6 inches! As a legal professional, he's also stood tall for human rights, good governance, and Intellectual Property law.

I know J.D. will work hard to bring the values of fairness and civility to Congress as he fights for hardworking families across the great state of Iowa. I know he will perform his duties with respect and compassion for every citizen even when they disagree -- something that is desperately needed in Washington. We need to get back to politics with purpose, and J.D. will help us get there.

Pundits say this is a "safe Republican" seat -- but that's what they said about Conor Lamb too! [**_Can you join me and help bold Democrat J.D. Scholten defeat Steve King in November?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/1082411/6060208/958502185?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4M2EwNTkyNS04MTQ1LWU4MTEtODBjMy0wMDE1NWRhNzg1ZTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HHGURJ3gZUyjIgY34E2MCPHZHkX3aIqKXT3s0P4cV9k=) You'll also be supporting our broader efforts to take back the House!

Thanks for all you do,

Tim

name: Team Gillum
subject: re: Boldfaced denial
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30e6/9e00690/444a91e8/24027ba7/2910776072/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180422_EarthD_acq_gq_act__TG&source=em18_180422_EarthD_acq_gq_act__TG")
Friends --

The week that Donald Trump was withdrawing the United States from the Paris Climate Accord, Andrew Gillum was breaking ground on a 120-acre solar farm that will triple Tallahassee's solar energy capacity.

From his opposition to the Sabal Trail and Keystone XL pipelines to his support of publicly owned renewable energy, Andrew is a proven leader in the fight to protect our environment. Meanwhile, one of our opponents voted for the Keystone XL pipeline and has taken more than $100,000 from a developer whose company has been fined millions of dollars for polluting Florida's environment.

**That 's why our team is proud to announce that 350 Action, a leading national organization working to change the politics of climate change, endorsed Andrew! [Check out what they had to say, then add your name to join our fight:](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30e6/9e00690/444a91e8/24027ba6/2910776072/VEsE/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-fight-for-our-environment?utm_campaign=em18_180422_EarthD_acq_gq_act__TG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180422_EarthD_acq_gq_act__TG&utm_content=2+-+Check+out+what+they+had+to+say+then+add+")**
_" Florida needs a governor who meets the rising seas and hurricane floodwaters with bold climate action, not boldfaced denial. As governor, Gillum would help bring a wave of green jobs, fostering ground-up solutions for the whole Sunshine State." \-- May Boeve, Executive Director_
Listen, if we want our children and grandchildren to experience the wonders our state has to offer, we must start making real strides to protect our environment. It's time Floridians have a leader with a proven record of standing up against Trump's attacks on our environment -- and will ensure that future generations can enjoy our planet's resources.

Andrew Gillum is more than ready to be that leader.

**[So in honor of Earth Day tomorrow, join 350 Action and Andrew's fight against polluters, and protect our environment today.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30e6/9e00690/444a91e8/24027bb9/2910776072/VEsF/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-fight-for-our-environment?utm_campaign=em18_180422_EarthD_acq_gq_act__TG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180422_EarthD_acq_gq_act__TG&utm_content=3+-+So+in+honor+of+Earth+Day+tomorrow+join+3")**

Thank you,

Team Gillum

**[TAKE ACTION](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30e6/9e00690/444a91e8/24027bb8/2910776072/VEsC/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-fight-for-our-environment?utm_campaign=em18_180422_EarthD_acq_gq_act__TG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180422_EarthD_acq_gq_act__TG&utm_content=4+-+TAKE+ACTION")**

body:
Got a question about what it's like to work in hyper-partisan Washington? Why I voted against the Republican health care overhaul? Where you can find the best pork tenderloin in Indiana? Something els |
---

| | Hi Peter,

My biggest priority in this campaign has always been folks like you. In the Senate, I don't work for the special interests -- I cast votes based on what's right for Hoosiers. **And I always, always,_always_ want to hear your thoughts.**

Last summer, when I kicked off my re-election campaign with a six-day RV tour, we heard firsthand from Hoosiers about what they want to see done in the Senate. So before I hit the road again in early May, I wanted to tell you that we're adding a little something extra to my trip! **This time, while I 've got downtime on the RV in between stops, I'll be answering your questions both via email and on a special Facebook Live session (details on that coming soon).**

What do you think? Got a question about what it's like to work in hyper-partisan Washington? Why I voted against the Republican health care overhaul? Where you can find the best pork tenderloin in Indiana? Something else? **[Submit it here, and I'll be reading and responding to a selection from the RV in May.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c20e5/7377f838/3e63d3d5/51faa490/2578555180/VEsH/ "https://donnelly.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/submit-your-question-for-joe-")** (Don't worry, Mike will be the one driving.)

name: Mike Stack
subject: Shoot first? Ask questions later? Hell no.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Committee to Elect Mike Stack
body:
| [](http://action.stackforpa.com/page/m/5fc049e1/7da2b91d/5cf0a87e/5c668e0b/2200950005/VEsH/ "http://www.stackforpa.com")
---

| | As Lieutenant Governor and as a Captain in the Pennsylvania National Guard, I've been privileged to work alongside active servicemembers and our nation's veterans. **They represent the best of us. But the country they served is letting them down.** **I have grave concerns over John Bolton becoming National Security Advisor, and Mike Pompeo's nomination to be Secretary of State. ** Pennsylvania's servicemembers and their families deserve the certainty of mature foreign policy leadership, not a government run by warmongers who have proven they shoot first and ask questions later. **When it comes to our servicemembers and veterans, I have two major criteria: that we respect their active service, and support them as their tours finish and they reenter civilian life. ** Veteran unemployment is higher than the national average. Too many former service members face medical challenges, food and housing insecurity, and substance abuse problems. We can do so much more to ensure that veterans returning to civilian life can find good-paying jobs, education opportunities, and utilize every benefit they earned through service. But that's made harder with reckless, war-first foreign policy. That's why, as Lieutenant Governor, I work every day to honor the veterans who have sacrificed so much for our nation's safety and security. And why I created the first ever Lieutenant Governor's Veteran's Task Force to deal with issues affecting veterans, from PTSD to homelessness and opioid addiction. [**There's less than a month until Pennsylvania's primary election. name: Jay Inslee
subject: Providing a lifeline
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
We must reverse severely restrictive new refugee policies. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79918/2f5ea88d/721f2f88/2cbcae0c/3275537469/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/end-restrictions-on-refugees?utm_source=ema_2018-04-20_refugees&source=ema_2018-04-20_refugees&utm_campaign=refugees&utm_medium=ema") | _" Immigration policy should be generous; it should be fair; it should be flexible. With such a policy we can turn to the world, and to our own past, with clean hands and a clear conscience."_ \- John F. Kennedy, _A Nation of Immigrants_
---
Friend,
John F. Kennedy's vision for America's immigration policy continues to inspire to this day. I wish D.C. politicians would heed his call.
I was reminded of these words when President Trump ordered airstrikes on Syria after Syrian government leaders launched a suspected chemical weapons attack that killed at least 70 people and injured hundreds more.
President Trump said his action was "about humanity."
But the contrast between the president's words and actions could not be starker. It's exactly what we've come to expect from him -- all talk (and tweets) and no action.
[**President Trump refuses to make America a place that welcomes more refugees that need a safe place to call home -- join me and demand a more welcoming refugee policy.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79918/2f5ea88d/721f2f88/2cbcae0c/3275537469/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/end-restrictions-on-refugees?utm_source=ema_2018-04-20_refugees&source=ema_2018-04-20_refugees&utm_campaign=refugees&utm_medium=ema")
Under President Trump, the U.S. Department of State has imposed severely restrictive new refugee policies in light of the historically high number of people fleeing violence in countries such as Syria. The outcome? As of today, only 11 Syrians have been admitted to the U.S. in 2018.
If the president truly believes this is about humanity, then he will swiftly review and reverse the current policies restricting the ability of qualified refugees for admission into the United States. That would be true humanity.
[**Join me on calling on President Trump and the State Department to renew our American commitment to supporting those in need during a time of crisis. Add your name here.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79918/2f5ea88d/721f2f88/2cbcae0c/3275537469/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/end-restrictions-on-refugees?utm_source=ema_2018-04-20_refugees&source=ema_2018-04-20_refugees&utm_campaign=refugees&utm_medium=ema")
America's policies should keep our country secure, and our values upheld -- today, Trump is failing to meet either one of those tests.
As John F. Kennedy wrote, then "we can turn to the world...with clean hands and a clean conscience."
Very truly yours,
Jay Inslee

With such a policy we can turn to the world, and to our own past, with clean hands and a clear conscience." \- John F. Kennedy, A Nation of Immigrants Friend, John F. Kennedy's vision for America's immigration policy continues to inspire to this day. I wish D.C. politicians would heed his call. I was reminded of these words when President Trump ordered airstrikes on Syria after Syrian government leaders launched a suspected chemical weapons attack that killed at least 70 people and injured hundreds more. President Trump said his action was "about humanity." But the contrast between the president's words and actions could not be starker. It's exactly what we've come to expect from him -- all talk (and tweets) and no action. "" Under President Trump, the U.S. Department of State has imposed severely restrictive new refugee policies in light of the historically high number of people fleeing violence in countries such as Syria. The outcome? As of today, only 11 Syrians have been admitted to the U.S. in 2018. If the president truly believes this is about humanity, then he will swiftly review and reverse the current policies restricting the ability of qualified refugees for admission into the United States. That would be true humanity. "" America's policies should keep our country secure, and our values upheld -- today, Trump is failing to meet either one of those tests. As John F. Kennedy wrote, then "we can turn to the world...with clean hands and a clean conscience." Very truly yours, Jay Inslee ""
